professionalSummary: Versatile Data Analyst & Scientist with 5+ years of experience in designing and engineering end-to-end data systems. Proven track record in automating data pipelines, developing high-impact dashboards, and leveraging cloud infrastructure to drive actionable business insights. Highly adaptable with a strong focus on technical scalability, efficiency, and solving complex data challenges.

experience.title: Data Analyst / Data Scientist
experience.company: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Centre (CIMMYT)
responsibilities: Built automated wheat disease surveillance pipeline (Google CSE API → newspaper3k extraction → GROQ LLM classification → SQL/Leaflet dashboards). Processed 2,744+ articles over 303 days; achieved 94% LLM classification success and ~10x noise reduction through iterative prompt engineering.

Standardised multi-country agronomy survey data: designed module structure and naming conventions across 200+ variables; retrospectively harmonised rice (2019–20) and wheat (2018–21) datasets. Published governance documentation used for ongoing CIMMYT form building.

Engineered data pipelines reducing manual processing by 70%+ via Python scheduling, SQL transformations, and dbt workflows. Deployed systems on VPS with Docker containerization and GitHub Actions CI/CD.

Designed and deployed dashboards (Power BI, Dash, Leaflet.js, Highcharts) translating complex agricultural datasets into actionable insights for researchers and government stakeholders across South Asia.

Led digital-first data collection pilots: evaluated tablet-based (KoBoToolbox), sensor-based, and hybrid approaches; selected and scaled cost-optimal solution for groundwater monitoring (gw-nepal.com).

experience.title: Data Manager
responsibilities: Developed and maintained robust data management frameworks for multi-disciplinary research projects, ensuring high-quality reporting for internal and external stakeholders.
Built custom scripts and interactive dashboards to monitor project performance, translating raw data into actionable insights for evaluation teams.

skills: Data Visualization (Dash, Power BI, Leaflet.js, Highcharts)

certifications.name: Introduction to Data Science in Python
Applied Plotting, Charting & Data Representation in Python

publications.title: The optimization of conservation agriculture practices requires attention to location-specific performance

projects.title: Wheat Disease Monitoring Pipeline
projects.description: Automated news surveillance system for South Asia (Google CSE → LLM classification → interactive dashboards). Processed 2,744 articles over 10 months; semantic filtering via GROQ LLM achieved 94% classification success, reducing noise by ~10x. Deployed on VPS with Docker + Python scheduling.

details.problem: Manual daily scraping and classification of wheat disease news from South Asian sources, with manual entry of location and GPS data. Time-consuming, error-prone, and no systematic tracking of outbreaks.
approach: Built automated pipeline triggered by Google Custom Search Engine API every 12 hours, searching niche wheat-disease keywords across select publishers
Extracted article content using newspaper3k library, filtering dead URLs and paywalled content upstream (zero cost)

Classified articles using GROQ LLM with wheat-disease-expert prompt engineering to extract: article type (advisory/monitoring/sighting/warning/others), country, district, disease, affected variety, and sighting date
Stored gold-standard classified data in SQLite with SQL geolookups to populate latitude/longitude from existing GPS datasets
Visualized results via Leaflet.js (regional maps) and Highcharts.js (temporal trends)
outcomes: Processed 2,744+ articles over 303 days; 1,364 extracted and sent to LLM for semantic filtering
Achieved 94% LLM classification success rate (only 221 of 1,364 failed processing); identified 36 true disease signals from 1,105 noise articles
~10x noise reduction through iterative search-layer tuning (removed research-paper spam and off-topic results)
10 months of unattended production operation surviving international relocation
Zero extraction-stage leakage: provably clean state machine (767 dead URLs = 767 unextracted)

projects.title: LCAS Data Standardisation & Governance
projects.description: Standardized naming conventions and module structure for multi-year, multi-country agronomy surveys (wheat & rice). Harmonized legacy datasets (2018–2021) and published definitive documentation enabling cross-survey interoperability. Still used in CIMMYT form building.

details.problem: Multi-year agronomy survey data collected inconsistently across projects and years with varying variable naming conventions (e.g., rice_yield vs yield_rice). Prevented cross-survey comparison, cross-temporal analysis, and reuse of data collection tools. Each year involved rewriting forms from scratch, losing built-in validation logic.
approach: Designed modular variable grouping structure with standardized naming conventions (200+ variables harmonized)
Ran homogenization protocol retrospectively across all legacy survey files (rice 2019–20, wheat 2018–21)
Established data governance documentation and published definitive module reference at systems-agronomy.github.io/lcas
Enabled reusable form templates and consistent data collection methodology going forward
outcomes: Harmonized rice dataset (2019–20) published on CIMMYT Dataverse with standardized variable naming
Harmonized wheat dataset (2018–21) published on CIMMYT Dataverse, enabling multi-year trend analysis
Data governance documentation still actively used by CIMMYT teams for new form building and survey design
Enabled cross-survey interoperability and elimination of manual data-cleaning time

projects.title: Groundwater Monitoring Dashboard
projects.description: Digital data collection system (KoBoToolbox/ODK) replacing paper-based field surveys in Nepal. Built Django/Python dashboards for real-time well location tracking and monthly trend analysis. Eliminated manual data-entry errors and improved transparency.

details.problem: Manual paper-based groundwater data collection from wells in Nepal, followed by manual re-entry into Excel. Error-prone (missing units, transcription errors), non-traceable (paper records lost), and difficult to share insights with stakeholders in real-time.
approach: Evaluated three collection approaches: tablet-based digital forms, semi-automated HOBOware sensors, fully automated cloud-connected sensors
Selected tablet-based KoBoToolbox/KoBo Collect as cost-optimal balance of data quality and operational feasibility
Digitized paper survey into XLSForm with in-form validation checks (flagged suspicious measurements during collection, not post-hoc)
Built Django/Python dashboard visualizing well locations (geographic), monthly groundwater trends, and measurement consistency
Trained local data collectors to use the Kobo Collect app, minimizing friction by keeping the field methodology unchanged
outcomes: Eliminated manual data re-entry errors and transcription mistakes; zero measurement validation failures in field
Increased transparency: field staff accountability tracked via app logs (confirms who collected data and when)
Successfully expanded from western Nepal (original scope) to eastern Nepal with trained data collectors
Honest challenges: stakeholder transition (original partner organization dissolved) required retraining on both collection and dashboard; server costs were an ongoing constraint

projects.title: Cricket Ball Tracking System
projects.description: Real-time sports video analysis: two-stage ML pipeline (camera-angle classifier + YOLO ball detection). Custom-trained YOLOv8 models on 374 labeled frames with 10ms inference latency on Apple Silicon. Built annotation tools, automated training, and video export pipeline.

details.problem: Analyzing cricket broadcast video to track ball position frame-by-frame is computationally expensive and requires robust real-time detection. Ball detection alone isn't enough — camera angle (bowling vs non-bowling perspective) changes detection strategy.
approach: Designed two-stage pipeline: (1) YOLOv8 classifier for camera-angle detection (bowling vs other), (2) YOLOv8 detector for ball localization
Built custom video annotation tool (mouse-based bounding-box drawing) for rapid dataset collection
Trained YOLOv8 nano models on 374 labeled cricket ball frames and 170 angle-classification frames
Implemented hand-crafted feature extraction baseline (histogram, edge density, brightness, texture) using Random Forest classifier for comparison
Integrated models into unified prediction pipeline with video export (overlay bounding boxes and confidence scores)
outcomes: Achieved ~10ms per-frame inference latency on Apple Silicon, enabling real-time 25fps processing
Created reproducible training pipeline with documented model trade-offs (YOLO vs Random Forest: accuracy vs speed vs stability)
Built clean CLI interface with mode switching (collect/predict) and model selection
Established documented roadmap for future enhancements (web UI, bounce detection, bird's eye view)

projects.title: Cricket Garden
projects.description: Data-driven cricket statistics platform with interactive analysis and visualizations.

details.problem: Cricket analysis often relies on surface-level stats or opinion. A unified platform for analytical cricket writing backed by curated data and interactive visualizations was missing.
approach: Built database of cricket statistics (player, team, match records)
Developed interactive visualization platform for exploring trends and relationships
Published original analytical pieces on cricket topics using the platform's data
outcomes: Platform hosting analytical cricket writing and visualizations
Demonstrated ability to combine data, analysis, and storytelling for a niche audience
